prompt: An endpoint checks only `ticket.owner_id == user.id`. Admins and team leads, who are legitimately allowed broader access, are wrongly blocked. What is the correct fix?
choices: Let any logged-in staff member skip the ownership check entirely
Combine role with relationship: grant by role scoped to the right ownership or team
Remove the ownership check so everyone can read every ticket
Hide the blocked tickets in the user interface only

prompt: An authorization function throws an unexpected error while evaluating a permission. Which two behaviours reflect a 'fail closed' design?
choices: Deny the request when the check cannot be completed
Treat a missing or null permission value as 'not allowed'
Allow the request so the user is not inconvenienced
Skip the check and log a warning for later review

prompt: Why is enforcing a row-level access rule once in the database (e.g. a row policy) often safer than re-implementing it in every application endpoint?
choices: Database checks are always faster than application checks
A single authoritative rule applies to every query, so no endpoint can forget it
Application code cannot read user identity
It removes the need to authenticate users at all
